notes_md:
**thẳng** = straight (not curved or bent). It describes shape or direction.

As an adjective, it describes things like hair, lines, roads, posture, or your back. It can also act like an adverb to mean “straight/directly” in movement or communication.

It also means “frank / straightforward” when talking about personality or speaking style (nói thẳng = speak honestly and directly).

Strong contrast: **thẳng** (straight) vs **cong** (curved, bent).
exampleSentences: Anh đi thẳng đến cuối con đường rồi rẽ trái nhé.
Em thích tóc của chị vì nó rất thẳng và mượt.
Chúng ta cần vẽ một đường thẳng trên tờ giấy này.
notes:
thẳng = straight (not curved or bent). It describes shape or direction.

As an adjective, it describes things like hair, lines, roads, posture, or your back. It can also act like an adverb to mean “straight/directly” in movement or communication.

It also means “frank / straightforward” when talking about personality or speaking style (nói thẳng = speak honestly and directly).

Strong contrast: thẳng (straight) vs cong (curved, bent).
exampleSentencesTranslated: Go straight to the end of the road and then turn left.
I like your hair because it is very straight and smooth.
We need to draw a straight line on this paper.
